## Reviewer's Guide Hardens SIMA fleet flatten and stop-sync flows against NT8 TriggerCustomEvent/order-operation quirks by adding guarded exception handling, synchronous fallback flatten paths, and emergency flatten behavior for stop failures, while refactoring FlattenAll into explicit phases with targeted error handling and clarified logging. #### Sequence diagram for SIMA fleet flatten dispatch with TriggerCustomEvent fallback ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Strategy participant NT8 as NT8Engine participant Queue as PendingFlattenOps participant Account Strategy->>Queue: Enqueue FlattenWorkItem Strategy->>NT8: TriggerCustomEvent(PumpFlattenOps) alt TriggerCustomEvent succeeds NT8->>Strategy: invoke PumpFlattenOps() Strategy->>Account: ProcessFlattenWorkItem_CancelOrders(workItem, acct) Strategy->>Account: ProcessFlattenWorkItem_ClosePositions(workItem, acct) Strategy->>Strategy: SetExpectedPositionLocked(ExpKey(acct.Name), 0) else InvalidOperationException TriggerCustomEvent Strategy->>Queue: _pendingFlattenOps.TryDequeue(out item) loop for each drained workItem Strategy->>Account: ProcessFlattenWorkItem_CancelOrders(workItem, acct) alt !workItem.CancelOnly Strategy->>Account: ProcessFlattenWorkItem_ClosePositions(workItem, acct) end Strategy->>Strategy: SetExpectedPositionLocked(ExpKey(acct.Name), 0) end else Unexpected Exception Strategy->>Queue: _pendingFlattenOps.TryDequeue(out item) loop for each drained workItem Strategy->>Account: ProcessFlattenWorkItem_CancelOrders(workItem, acct) alt !workItem.CancelOnly Strategy->>Account: ProcessFlattenWorkItem_ClosePositions(workItem, acct) end Strategy->>Strategy: SetExpectedPositionLocked(ExpKey(acct.Name), 0) end end ``` #### Sequence diagram for phased FlattenAll with guarded steps ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant User participant Strategy participant SIMA as SIMAFleet User->>Strategy: FlattenAll() Strategy->>Strategy: IsMasterAccount() / IsTradingEnabled() Strategy->>Strategy: Print("FLATTEN: Closing all positions...") rect rgb(230,230,230) Strategy->>Strategy: CancelMasterEntryOrders() alt InvalidOperationException CancelOrder Strategy->>Strategy: Print("WARNING: Known quirk in CancelMasterEntryOrders") else Exception Strategy->>Strategy: Print("CRITICAL: Unexpected exception in CancelMasterEntryOrders") end end rect rgb(230,230,230) alt EnableSIMA Strategy->>SIMA: DispatchFleetFlatten() alt InvalidOperationException DispatchFleetFlatten Strategy->>Strategy: Print("WARNING: Known quirk in DispatchFleetFlatten") else Exception Strategy->>Strategy: Print("CRITICAL: Unexpected exception in DispatchFleetFlatten") end end end rect rgb(230,230,230) Strategy->>Strategy: ResetSyncStateAndPurgeFollowers() Strategy->>Strategy: FlattenFilledMasterPositions() Strategy->>Strategy: CancelUnfilledMasterEntries() end Strategy-->User: FlattenAll complete (even if some phases warn) ``` ### File-Level Changes | Change | Details | Files | | ------ | ------- | ----- | | Add guarded exception handling and synchronous fallback flattening when SIMA async TriggerCustomEvent dispatch fails. |
  • Wrap FlattenAllApexAccounts TriggerCustomEvent with InvalidOperationException filter for known TriggerCustomEvent quirks and drain/process the pending flatten queue synchronously without rethrowing.
  • Add generic Exception catch in FlattenAllApexAccounts that logs CRITICAL details, drains the queue, performs synchronous flatten per account, and avoids rethrow to keep positions protected.
  • Apply similar TriggerCustomEvent guarded handling and synchronous fallback flatten logic in ChainNextFlattenOp, including remaining-accounts messaging and non-rethrow behavior.

### Pull Request Overview

This PR successfully implements the phased flatten and stop-synchronization logic required to harden SIMA operations against NinjaTrader 8 quirks. However, the current implementation contains a critical race condition where the flattening guard is released before the synchronous fallback logic completes, potentially allowing concurrent strategy events to interfere with account protection.

While Codacy marks the PR as 'up to standards', there is a substantial increase in cyclomatic complexity (delta +32 in one file) and significant logic duplication across four recovery paths. Furthermore, none of the critical acceptance criteria involving fallback triggers or emergency flatten scenarios are verified by new tests, presenting a significant verification gap for such high-risk operational changes.

#### About this PR
- The PR description is missing critical project metadata, including Mission context and Build Tags. Additionally, the Mandatory Gates checklist is entirely unchecked, and no unit/integration tests were provided to verify the fallback or emergency flatten logic.



#### Test suggestions
- [ ] Missing recommended test scenario: TriggerCustomEvent failure during fleet flatten triggers the synchronous fallback queue drain.
- [ ] Missing recommended test scenario: Order operation (e.g., CancelOrder) failure during account flatten allows processing to continue to next account.
- [ ] Missing recommended test scenario: Stop order creation failure (e.g., InvalidOperationException) triggers immediate emergency FlattenPositionByName.
- [ ] Missing recommended test scenario: Exception during Phase 1 of FlattenAll verifies that Phase 2 (DispatchFleetFlatten) and Phase 3 (ResetSync) still execute.
- [ ] Missing recommended test scenario: ManageCIT handles ChangeOrder 'known quirk' exceptions without aborting the loop for other fleet accounts.
